varieties.name: Teddy Bear
varieties.traits: Compact pyramidal form, 4-5m tall, dense branching, glossy dark green leaves with rusty-brown felted undersides
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane's subtropical climate. Heat and humidity tolerant, handles afternoon sun well with adequate water. Popular for screening and hedging. Requires minimal pruning.
varieties.availability: Widely available - Magnolia King, Dawsons Garden World, Ross Evans Garden Centre QLD

varieties.name: Little Gem
varieties.traits: Dwarf form, 3-4m tall × 2-3m wide, narrow upright habit, glossy green leaves with rusty undersides
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous bloomer from late spring through summer
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for small Brisbane gardens. Heat tolerant, performs well in subtropical conditions. Suitable for containers. Protect from hot afternoon sun in exposed positions. Slow growing.
varieties.availability: Very common - Online Plants, Nursery Near Me, Nature Hills, Brisbane nurseries

varieties.name: Kay Parris
varieties.origin: Gilbert's Nursery, South Carolina, USA, early 1990s. Named for Katherine Killingsworth Parris
varieties.traits: Columnar upright form, 6-8m tall × 2-3m wide, dense pyramidal shape, glossy foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane subtropical conditions. Heat and humidity tolerant. Suitable for narrow spaces. RHS Award of Garden Merit. Fast establishment. Ideal for screening.
varieties.availability: Available - Sunshine Coast Nursery, specialized magnolia suppliers

varieties.name: D.D. Blanchard
varieties.traits: Large tree 15-18m tall × 8-10m wide, pyramidal form, very dark glossy green leaves with distinctive rusty-orange undersides

varieties.name: Bracken's Brown Beauty
varieties.traits: Medium tree 10-15m tall × 5-8m wide, dense pyramidal form, glossy green leaves with pronounced cinnamon-brown undersides
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy bloomer, fastest-growing magnolia grandiflora cultivar (up to 60cm/year when young)
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for Brisbane - thrives in subtropical heat and humidity. More cold-hardy than most but performs brilliantly in warm climates. Dense growth makes excellent screen. Relatively drought-tolerant once established.
varieties.availability: Available - The Tree Center, Nature Hills, Australian advanced tree suppliers

varieties.name: St Mary
varieties.traits: Compact form 4-6m tall, narrow pyramidal habit, glossy foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Bred specifically for Australian conditions including Brisbane's subtropical climate. Excellent heat and humidity tolerance. Suitable for urban gardens.

varieties.name: Exmouth
varieties.traits: Large tree to 12m tall, upright pyramidal form, glossy green foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant flowering, flowers at younger age than species

varieties.name: Super Gem
varieties.traits: Compact 4-5m tall, dense foliage, narrow form
varieties.growing_notes: Specifically bred for temperate to subtropical climates including Brisbane. Heat tolerant, suitable for screening and hedging. Handles light frost.
varieties.availability: Available - Sunshine Coast Nursery, QLD garden centers

varieties.name: Sweet Spire
varieties.traits: Narrow columnar habit 3-4m tall × 2m wide, dark green glossy leaves with rusty-brown felt underneath
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for Brisbane subtropical conditions. Narrow form ideal for screening and small gardens. Heat tolerant. Minimal maintenance required.

varieties.name: Coolwyn Gloss
varieties.traits: Pyramidal to rounded 4-6m tall × 2-3m wide, very glossy dark green leaves up to 20cm long
varieties.growing_notes: Thrives in warm temperate to subtropical Brisbane climate. Prefers full sun to partial shade. Can tolerate some drought once established. Excellent year-round foliage interest.

varieties.name: Elizabeth 🧬
varieties.origin: Brooklyn Botanic Garden, USA, 1956 (M. acuminata × M. denudata)
varieties.traits: Small tree 5m in 10 years, compact pyramidal form, dark green elliptic foliage
varieties.growing_notes: One of first yellow magnolias. Can be grown in Brisbane with afternoon shade and consistent water during summer. Hardier than M. denudata due to hybrid vigor. Later flowering reduces frost risk.
varieties.availability: Specialty magnolia nurseries, may require ordering

varieties.name: Butterflies
varieties.origin: Philip Savage, Blooming Hills Michigan, USA, 1988 (M. acuminata 'Fertile Myrtle' × M. denudata 'Sawada's Cream')
varieties.traits: Upright pyramidal 8-10m tall, dark green foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Thrives in both cold and heat - suitable for Brisbane with protection from extreme afternoon sun. Non-fading yellow color. Award of Garden Merit (UK) 1993. Plant in cool sheltered position.
varieties.availability: Specialty nurseries, may need advance order

varieties.name: Soulangeana (Saucer Magnolia) 🧬
varieties.origin: Étienne Soulange-Bodin, France, 1820s (M. denudata × M. liliiflora)
varieties.traits: Small tree or large shrub 6-8m tall × 6-8m wide, broad spreading form

varieties.name: Ann
varieties.traits: Compact shrub 3-3.5m tall × 3-4.5m wide, multi-stemmed
varieties.growing_notes: Blooms 2-4 weeks later than standard magnolias, reducing frost damage. Can grow in Brisbane with cool position and summer water. Tolerates air pollution.

varieties.name: Betty
varieties.traits: Rounded shrub 4-4.5m tall × 6m wide, wider than 'Ann'
varieties.growing_notes: Fast-growing for Little Girl series. Flowers remain cup-shaped. Brisbane suitable with cool position and summer water. Late blooming reduces frost damage.

varieties.name: Jane
varieties.origin: US National Arboretum, 1965 (M. liliiflora 'Reflorescens' × M. stellata 'Waterlily')
varieties.traits: Tallest of Little Girl series, 5-6m tall, upright multi-stemmed tree form
varieties.growing_notes: More tree-like than other Little Girls. Brisbane suitable with cool site and consistent summer moisture. Late flowering avoids frost.

varieties.name: Susan
varieties.origin: US National Arboretum, 1965 (M. liliiflora 'Nigra' × M. stellata 'Rosea')
varieties.traits: Compact shrub to small tree 3-4m tall, slow-growing
varieties.yield_notes: Good flowering with possible repeat bloom
varieties.growing_notes: Late blooming reduces frost damage. Brisbane suitable with afternoon shade and regular summer water. Slow-growing makes good small garden specimen.

varieties.name: Port Wine Magnolia (M. figo) 🌱
varieties.origin: China, cultivated for centuries. Species described by Loureiro, reclassified by Sprengel
varieties.traits: Compact evergreen shrub 3-4m tall × 2-3m wide, dense bushy form, small glossy green leaves
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT for Brisbane subtropical climate. Thrives in heat and humidity. Perfect for hedging, screening, topiary. Slow-growing, low-maintenance. Tolerates light frost. Outstanding fragrance near outdoor living areas. 'Port Wine' cultivar has deeper rose to maroon flowers.
varieties.availability: Widely available - Dawsons Garden World, Sunshine Coast Nursery, Bamboo Land, Online Plants, Pot Plant Heaven Perth

varieties.name: M. figo var. crassipes
varieties.traits: Larger variety, can reach 5m, glossy foliage
varieties.growing_notes: More vigorous than figo. Excellent for Brisbane subtropical conditions. Used in breeding 'Stellar Ruby' hybrid. Heat and humidity tolerant.

varieties.name: M. figo var. skinneriana
varieties.traits: More robust than standard figo, larger leaves

varieties.name: Magnolia stellata (Star Magnolia) 🌱
varieties.traits: Compact shrub or small tree 4.5-6m tall, rounded crown

varieties.name: Magnolia liliiflora 'Nigra' 🧬
varieties.origin: Cultivar of Chinese native M. liliiflora, ancient cultivation
varieties.traits: Spreading deciduous shrub or small tree 3-4m, rounded habit
varieties.growing_notes: Historic cultivar, parent of many hybrids including Little Girl series. Can grow in Brisbane with afternoon shade and summer water. Later flowering reduces frost damage.

varieties.name: Magnolia denudata (Yulan Magnolia) 🌱
varieties.origin: China, cultivated for over 1,400 years in Chinese temple gardens
varieties.traits: Small deciduous tree to 5m, spreading form
varieties.growing_notes: Ancient Chinese ornamental. Can be grown in Brisbane with cool sheltered position. Parent of many hybrid magnolias including soulangeana. Less heat-tolerant than evergreen species but possible with care.

varieties.name: Magnolia acuminata (Cucumber Tree) 🌱
varieties.traits: Large deciduous tree to 20m+, pyramidal form, pointed leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Source of yellow color in modern hybrids (Elizabeth, Butterflies). Interesting cucumber-shaped seed pods. Can be challenging in Brisbane but important historically. Subspecies subcordata has better yellow flowers.

varieties.name: Magnolia macrophylla (Bigleaf Magnolia) 🌱
varieties.origin: Southeastern USA and Mexico, native species
varieties.traits: Small tree to 12m, enormous leaves up to 90cm long creating subtropical appearance
varieties.growing_notes: Massive leaves and flowers. Can be attempted in Brisbane in very sheltered position with protection from wind. Requires consistent moisture. Creates dramatic tropical effect.

varieties.name: Magnolia virginiana (Sweetbay Magnolia) 🌱
varieties.origin: Eastern USA coastal areas, native species. First magnolia scientifically described (type species)
varieties.traits: Small tree or large shrub 6-9m, semi-evergreen to deciduous, silvery leaf undersides

varieties.name: Galaxy
varieties.origin: William Kosar, US National Arboretum, 1963 (M. liliiflora 'Nigra' × M. sprengeri 'Diva'), released 1980
varieties.traits: Small tree with single leader, upright pyramidal becoming more spreading with age, 6-8m
varieties.growing_notes: Late flowering usually avoids frost. Suitable for Brisbane with cool position. Can be grown in streets and confined sites. Easily rooted from cuttings. Highly rated by European growers.

varieties.name: Genie
varieties.origin: 1930s, Wyoming Nurseries, Ohio (M. stellata × M. liliiflora)
varieties.traits: Compact free-flowering medium shrub 3-4.5m
varieties.growing_notes: Compact size good for small gardens. RHS AGM in Britain. Can be attempted in Brisbane with cool position and summer water. Extended flowering period.

varieties.name: Felix
varieties.origin: Jury Magnolias, New Zealand, modern release
varieties.traits: Small tree, vigorous growth
varieties.growing_notes: One of New Zealand's premier modern magnolia introductions. Massive flowers. May be suitable for Brisbane cool positions with protection.

varieties.name: Black Tulip
varieties.traits: Compact tree
varieties.growing_notes: Outstanding dark flower color. New Zealand breeding. May be attempted in Brisbane with cool sheltered position.

varieties.name: Vulcan 🧬
varieties.origin: Jury Magnolias, New Zealand (M. liliiflora hybrid × M. campbellii 'Lanarth')
varieties.traits: Small tree to 5m
varieties.growing_notes: Distinctive wine-red color. Historic New Zealand hybrid. Can be attempted in Brisbane with cool sheltered position and summer water. Mature plants take 3 years after planting to flower.

varieties.name: Caerhays Belle
varieties.origin: Dutch breeding (M. sprengeri 'Diva' × M. sargentiana var. robusta)
varieties.traits: Small tree
varieties.growing_notes: Outstanding European hybrid. May be challenging in Brisbane subtropical heat but possible with very cool sheltered position.

varieties.name: Phillip Tregunna
varieties.origin: Hybrid M. campbellii × M. sargentiana var. robusta
varieties.traits: Small to medium tree
varieties.growing_notes: Exceptional hybrid. Very challenging in Brisbane subtropical conditions but may be attempted by experienced growers in ideal microclimate.

varieties.name: Victoria
varieties.traits: Medium tree 6-8m tall × 4-5.5m wide, broad spreading form, cold-hardy foliage
varieties.growing_notes: Developed for cold climates but adapts well to Brisbane subtropical heat. Good frost resistance but also handles humidity. Popular in Pacific Northwest and Central Europe.

varieties.name: Edith Bogue
varieties.traits: Large tree 12-18m tall × 6-9m wide, pyramidal to rounded, strong branches
varieties.growing_notes: Known for cold hardiness but also performs well in Brisbane subtropical conditions. Strong branches resist breakage. Lighter green foliage with reddish-brown undersides.

varieties.name: Claudia Wannamaker
varieties.traits: Massive tree 15m+ tall × 9m+ wide, ornate picturesque form with age
varieties.growing_notes: Develops more branches and ornate appearance with maturity. Suitable for large Brisbane properties with space. Heat and humidity tolerant.

varieties.name: Samuel Sommer
varieties.traits: Large tree
varieties.growing_notes: Famous for exceptionally large flowers. Suitable for Brisbane with adequate space. Heat tolerant.

varieties.name: Alta
varieties.traits: Tall and narrow growth habit, excellent for hedges and screening
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for Brisbane screening applications. Narrow form fits tight spaces. Heat and humidity tolerant.

varieties.name: Hasse
varieties.traits: Upright grower, thrives in tight spaces
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for confined urban Brisbane gardens. Upright habit suits courtyards. Featured at Swarthmore College. Heat tolerant.

varieties.name: Greenback
varieties.traits: 5-6m tall × 3-4m wide, more compact than species
varieties.growing_notes: Australian-bred for local conditions including Brisbane subtropical climate. More compact than species, suitable for smaller gardens.

varieties.name: Green Giant
varieties.traits: Fast-growing statement tree, large pyramidal form
varieties.growing_notes: Rapid growth rate for magnolia. Suitable for Brisbane with adequate space. Creates impressive landscape presence quickly.

varieties.name: Magnolia champaca (Champaca, Joy Perfume Tree) 🌱 🏆
varieties.origin: Native to India (including Kerala), Java, Philippines, Southeast Asia. Sanskrit name 'campaka'. Cultivated for millennia
varieties.traits: Large evergreen tree 15-20m tall (can reach 30m), tapering conical to cylindrical crown, glossy bright green veined leaves 10-30cm
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS TO KERALA. Regarded as one of most sacred trees of India and tropical Asia. Flowers used in religious ceremonies, temple offerings, garlands. Essential oil extracted for perfume industry. Thrives in Kerala's tropical monsoon climate - ideal heat, humidity, and rainfall. Can be grown in containers with pruning. Seeds dispersed by birds. Known locally as Sampangi in South India.
varieties.availability: Widely cultivated - Local Kerala nurseries, Plantshub India, Indian garden centers, temple gardens. Seeds and plants readily available.

varieties.name: Magnolia × alba (White Champaka, Pak-Lan) 🧬 🏆
varieties.origin: Cultivated origin (M. champaca × M. montana hybrid), Southeast Asia and China, introduced to Taiwan 17th century. Cultivated for centuries
varieties.traits: Medium-sized evergreen tree to 30m, smooth silvery-grey bark, elliptical leaves 10-35cm × 4-11cm
varieties.growing_notes: CULTURALLY SIGNIFICANT IN KERALA. White version of champaca. Flowers woven into garlands for traditional ceremonies, weddings, religious offerings in Indonesia and Thailand. Essential oil extracted for perfume industry (200+ patents). Known as 'Bai Yu Lan' (white-jade flower) in China. Thrives perfectly in Kerala's tropical monsoon climate. Sterile hybrid propagated vegetatively. Popular street tree.
varieties.availability: Widely available - Kerala nurseries, Indian garden centers, flower markets during season

varieties.name: Golden Magnolia (Simhachalam Sampenga) 🌱
varieties.origin: India, associated with Simhachalam temple near Vishakhapatnam
varieties.traits: Small to medium evergreen tree

varieties.name: Green Magnolia (Aaku Sampenga/Manoranjitam) 🌱
varieties.traits: Small to medium evergreen tree, can grow to 3m (10 feet)

varieties.name: White Sampangi (Teega Sampangi) 🌱
varieties.traits: Small evergreen tree or large shrub

varieties.name: Magnolia doltsopa (Sweet Michelia, Temple Tree) 🌱
varieties.origin: Eastern Himalayas, Meghalaya subtropical forests (India), native 1500-2400m elevation
varieties.traits: Large shrub to tree 20-30m, leathery dark-green leaves 6-17cm length
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE TO INDIA including Meghalaya. Growing in popularity as street tree in coastal California. Prefers higher elevations (1500-2400m) but can adapt to Kerala's lower elevation tropical climate with adequate moisture. Best in areas with mean annual rainfall 3000-4000mm (Kerala receives heavy monsoon). Tolerates 5-45°C. Wood harvested from wild.
varieties.availability: Limited in Kerala lowlands, better for hill stations, Indian botanical gardens

varieties.name: Magnolia nilagirica (Nilgiri Magnolia) 🌱 🏆
varieties.origin: ENDEMIC TO WESTERN GHATS OF INDIA including Agastya Mala, Megamalai Wildlife Sanctuary, Anaimalai Hills, Palni Hills
varieties.traits: Tree, threatened by habitat loss
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS TO KERALA REGION (Western Ghats). Reclassified under Magnolia in 2000. Excellent wood quality leads to severe logging threat. Has lost 40% of wild population in ~180 years. IUCN status: Vulnerable (2015). Forests being harvested faster than regrowth. Different common names in various Indian languages. Conservation priority for Kerala.
varieties.availability: Protected species - conservation programs, botanical gardens only, NOT for commercial cultivation

varieties.name: Magnolia cathcartii
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree with proleptic branching
varieties.growing_notes: Found in NE India. Part of Sino-Himalayan evergreen magnolia group. May be suitable for Kerala hill stations but challenging in lowland tropical heat.

varieties.name: M. griffithii, M. gustavii, M. pealiana 🌱
varieties.traits: Evergreen trees, Section Maingola
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS TO NE INDIA. Three species found in Northeast India. Part of tropical Asian magnolia diversity. Restricted to tropical Asia. Research and conservation interest.

varieties.name: Magnolia figo (Port Wine Magnolia, Banana Shrub) 🌱
varieties.traits: Compact evergreen shrub 3-5m, small glossy green leaves, dense bushy habit
varieties.growing_notes: EXCELLENT FOR KERALA. Thrives in tropical to subtropical climates. Perfect for Kerala's monsoon conditions - tolerates high heat and humidity. Also popular as houseplant. Can be grown in containers. Flowers covered with velvet-like hairs. 'Port Wine' cultivar has deeper rose-maroon flowers. Isoamyl alcohol provides banana scent. Slow-growing, low-maintenance.
varieties.availability: Available through specialty tropical plant importers to India, may require sourcing from international suppliers

varieties.name: M. figo var. crassipes
varieties.traits: Larger robust variety, can reach 5m+
varieties.growing_notes: More vigorous than standard figo. Excellent for Kerala's tropical monsoon climate. Used in breeding. Heat and humidity tolerant. Flowers covered with velvet-like hairs.
varieties.availability: Specialty tropical plant suppliers, international nurseries

varieties.name: Magnolia rajaniana (Peacock Magnolia, Champa Khao)
varieties.origin: Southeast Asia, variety of M. champaca
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree
varieties.growing_notes: Known as Champee Si Nuan or Magnolia Ivory. Thrives in tropical climates like Kerala. Similar care to champaca.

varieties.name: Magnolia coco (Dwarf Magnolia, Coconut Magnolia)
varieties.traits: Dwarf/compact form magnolia
varieties.growing_notes: Compact form suitable for smaller Kerala gardens. Tropical climate adapted. Also known as Liriodendron coco, Magnolia pumila.

varieties.name: Little Gem
varieties.traits: Dwarf form 3-4m tall, narrow upright habit, glossy green leaves with rusty undersides
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous bloomer late spring through summer
varieties.growing_notes: Can be adapted to Kerala with consistent water during monsoon and protection from extreme midday heat. Suitable for containers. Slow-growing. Best in partial shade in Kerala conditions.
varieties.availability: Plantshub India, may require special order/import

varieties.name: Teddy Bear
varieties.traits: Compact pyramidal 4-5m, dense branching, glossy dark green leaves with rusty-brown undersides
varieties.growing_notes: Heat and humidity tolerant. Can adapt to Kerala's tropical monsoon climate with adequate water and some afternoon shade. Popular worldwide in subtropical to tropical zones.
varieties.availability: May be available through Plantshub India or specialty importers

varieties.name: D.D. Blanchard
varieties.traits: Large tree 15-18m, pyramidal, very dark glossy leaves with rusty-orange undersides
varieties.growing_notes: Tolerates subtropical to tropical heat. Can be attempted in Kerala with adequate space, consistent monsoon moisture. Dramatic foliage contrast. Requires room to grow.

varieties.name: Kay Parris
varieties.origin: Gilbert's Nursery, South Carolina, USA, early 1990s
varieties.traits: Columnar upright 6-8m tall × 2-3m wide, dense pyramidal
varieties.growing_notes: Heat and humidity tolerant. RHS Award of Garden Merit. Can adapt to Kerala tropical conditions with consistent water. Suitable for narrow spaces.

varieties.name: Bracken's Brown Beauty
varieties.traits: Medium tree 10-15m, dense pyramidal, glossy leaves with cinnamon-brown undersides
varieties.growing_notes: Known for heat tolerance. Performs well in warm climates. Can adapt to Kerala with adequate monsoon moisture. Fast-growing provides quick results.

varieties.name: Magnolia × soulangeana (Saucer Magnolia) 🧬
varieties.traits: Small tree 6-8m, broad spreading form
varieties.growing_notes: Historic 1826 hybrid. Challenging in Kerala lowlands - best attempted in hill stations (Munnar, Wayanad) with cooler temperatures. Requires adequate chilling for dormancy. Available in India through Paudhshala.
varieties.availability: Paudhshala India - may be suitable for Kerala hill stations only

varieties.name: Magnolia stellata (Star Magnolia) 🌱
varieties.traits: Compact shrub 4.5-6m, rounded crown

varieties.name: Magnolia liliiflora 🌱
varieties.traits: Spreading deciduous shrub 3-4m
varieties.growing_notes: Parent of many hybrids. Challenging in Kerala except hill stations. Requires cooler climate and dormancy period. 'Nigra' cultivar most common.
varieties.availability: Very limited, hill station botanical gardens

varieties.name: Magnolia denudata (Yulan Magnolia) 🌱
varieties.traits: Small deciduous tree to 5m
varieties.growing_notes: Ancient Chinese ornamental. Not heat-tolerant. Only suitable for Kerala hill stations with significantly cooler climate. Parent of many hybrids.
varieties.availability: Very limited, botanical collections in cooler regions only

varieties.name: Magnolia compressa
varieties.traits: Evergreen tropical species
varieties.growing_notes: Tropical Asian species. May be suitable for Kerala's monsoon climate. Research interest.

varieties.name: Magnolia coriacea
varieties.traits: Evergreen with leathery leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Tropical species. Leathery foliage adapts to monsoon conditions. Potential for Kerala cultivation.

varieties.name: Magnolia foveolata
varieties.traits: Evergreen tropical species
varieties.growing_notes: Formerly M. fulgens and M. longistyla. Tropical Asian species potentially suitable for Kerala climate.

varieties.name: Magnolia hypolampra
varieties.traits: Evergreen tropical species
varieties.growing_notes: Formerly M. hedyosperma. Tropical species. Research interest for Kerala region.

varieties.name: Magnolia laevifolia
varieties.traits: Evergreen with smooth leaves
varieties.growing_notes: Tropical species. Smooth-leaved variety. Potential for tropical cultivation.

varieties.name: Magnolia montana
varieties.traits: Evergreen mountain species
varieties.growing_notes: Parent of M. × alba (white champaka). Mountain species may suit Kerala hill stations better than lowlands.

varieties.name: Magnolia xanthantha
varieties.traits: Evergreen tropical species
varieties.growing_notes: Tropical species. Name suggests yellow coloration. Research interest.

varieties.name: Magnolia insignis
varieties.origin: Warm-temperate to tropical Asia, Section Manglietia
varieties.traits: Evergreen, branching sylleptic, stipular scar 25-75% petiole length
varieties.growing_notes: Best-known in cultivation of Manglietia group. Important group in warm-temperate to tropical Asia. May be suitable for Kerala. Experimental cultivation.

varieties.name: Magnolia decidua
varieties.traits: DECIDUOUS (exceptional in Manglietia section which is mostly evergreen)
varieties.growing_notes: Unique deciduous member of otherwise evergreen Manglietia group. Tropical Asian species. May require some dormancy unsuitable for Kerala lowlands.

varieties.name: Magnolia delavayi
varieties.traits: Large bushy form, coarse foliage, sea-green leaves 12 inches × 6 inches of great size and substance
varieties.growing_notes: Part of Gwillimia group (nocturnal bloomers). Especially attractive in tropical settings near outdoor rooms, pools, entryways. Cultivated in coastal California. May adapt to Kerala with care. Dramatic foliage.

varieties.name: Magnolia yuyuanensis
varieties.traits: Compact evergreen tree
varieties.growing_notes: Hardy to USDA Zone 7 but may adapt to Kerala's tropical conditions. Compact size suitable for smaller gardens. On horizon for increased popularity.

varieties.name: Magnolia nilagirica (Endemic to Western Ghats, IUCN Vulnerable)
